title: Enforce the seven gates (P1-P7) via the CLI

acceptanceCriteria.text: All gate tests pass (P1/P4/P5/P6/P7) via node --test

title: feat: chalk decisions

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk decisions` prints the contents of .chalk/decisions.md
add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting it shows a decision after `chalk decision`

issue.body:
- [ ] `chalk decisions` prints the contents of .chalk/decisions.md
- [ ] add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting it shows a decision after `chalk decision`

findings.note: `decisions()` calls readFileSync(s.p.decisions) without a guard; if .chalk/decisions.md were missing it would throw an unfriendly error. In practice init always creates the file, so both criteria are satisfied.

Test only covers the happy path (decision exists). No assertion of clean behavior when the log is empty/just-initialized, but the criterion only requires showing a decision after `chalk decision`, which is covered.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** Add a tiny read-only `decisions` command to the `cmds` map in `bin/chalk.mjs` that prints `.chalk/decisions.md`. Mirror the existing `decision`/`lesson` pattern (`Store.open()` → `s.p.decisions`); `readFileSync` is already imported. Simplest, reuses existing store paths — no new utilities.

**Steps:**
1. In `bin/chalk.mjs`, after the `decision({...})` command (~line 875), add `decisions()` that does `const s = Store.open();` and `console.log(readFileSync(s.p.decisions, 'utf8'))` (guard with `existsSync` or fall back to empty — but `init` always creates the file, so a direct read is fine).
2. Add a one-line entry in `printHelp()` near the `chalk decision` line (~1011) for discoverability.

**Files:**
- `bin/chalk.mjs` — new `decisions` cmd in the `cmds` object (reuse `Store.open()` + `s.p.decisions` from `lib/store.mjs`; `readFileSync` already imported); help text.
- `test/protocol.test.mjs` — new test.

**Test:** Add `test('decisions — prints logged decisions', ...)`: `chalk(d,'init','--name','demo')`; `chalk(d,'decision','use sqlite for the ledger','--why','simplicity')`; then `const r = chalk(d,'decisions')`; assert `r.code===0` and `assert.match(r.out, /use sqlite for the ledger/)`. Fails today (unknown command exits 1), passes with the new command.

title: feat: chalk lesson list

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk lesson list` prints the recorded lessons from .chalk/lessons.md (the same memory injected into agents)
`chalk lesson \"...\"` (adding) still works unchanged
add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `lesson list` shows a previously-added lesson

issue.body:
- [ ] `chalk lesson list` prints the recorded lessons from .chalk/lessons.md (the same memory injected into agents)
- [ ] `chalk lesson \"...\"` (adding) still works unchanged
- [ ] add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `lesson list` shows a previously-added lesson

findings.note: `chalk lesson list` reads s.lessons(1000) while context injection uses s.lessons(15); criterion 1 says 'the same memory injected into agents'. They share the source file but the cap differs, so list could surface older lessons not actually injected. Harmless/arguably more complete, not blocking.

Dispatch treats `_[0] === 'list'` as the list subcommand, so a literal lesson text starting with the word 'list' (e.g. `chalk lesson list the deps`) would be misrouted. Edge case, acceptable for the contract.
The added test only checks the happy path (one added lesson appears). No test for the empty-state ('no lessons recorded yet.') branch, but criteria 1-3 are all directly exercised.

plan:
## Approach
Add a `list` subcommand to the existing `lesson` handler in `bin/chalk.mjs`: if `_[0] === 'list'`, print recorded lessons via the existing `Store.lessons()`; otherwise keep the current add behavior unchanged. Reuses the store reader that already feeds agent context — minimal, no new module.

## Steps
1. In `bin/chalk.mjs` `lesson({ _, flags })`, branch at top: `if (_[0] === 'list')` → open store, get `s.lessons(1000)`, print each line (and a friendly note if empty), then `return`.
2. Leave the existing add path (`_.join(' ') || flags.text` → `appendLesson`) untouched so `chalk lesson "..."` still works.
3. Add usage line for `chalk lesson list` in the help block (~line 1019).

## Files
- `bin/chalk.mjs` — `lesson()` handler (~line 885) + help text. Reuse `Store.open()` and `s.lessons()` from `lib/store.mjs`.
- `test/protocol.test.mjs` — new test.

## Test
Add to `test/protocol.test.mjs`: `init`, `chalk(d,'lesson','prefer reusing existing utilities')` (code 0), then `chalk(d,'lesson','list')` — assert `.code === 0` and `.out` matches `/prefer reusing existing utilities/`. Fails today because `lesson list` currently treats "list" as lesson text and never prints stored lessons.

title: feat: chalk log --grep <text>

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk log --grep <text>` shows only update events whose title contains <text> (case-insensitive), still honoring `--n`, `--type`, and `--reverse`
without `--grep`, output is unchanged
add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `--grep` filters AND that it composes with another flag (e.g. `--type`)

issue.body:
- [ ] `chalk log --grep <text>` shows only update events whose title contains <text> (case-insensitive), still honoring `--n`, `--type`, and `--reverse`
- [ ] without `--grep`, output is unchanged
- [ ] add a test in `test/protocol.test.mjs` asserting `--grep` filters AND that it composes with another flag (e.g. `--type`)

findings.note: The new test composes --grep with --type and verifies case-insensitivity + plain-unchanged, satisfying criterion 3. It does not assert --grep composes with --n or --reverse, but criterion 1's --n/--reverse behavior is structurally guaranteed (grep filters before slice(-n)/reverse, identical to the already-tested --type path).
String(u.title) coerces missing titles to 'undefined' which could spuriously match a grep for 'undefined', but update titles are always required in practice, so no realistic impact.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** Mirror the existing `--type` filter in `cmds.log` (bin/chalk.mjs:921) — add a `--grep` filter on `u.title` applied to the full set *before* `.slice(-n)`, so `--n`/`--reverse`/`--json` continue to compose unchanged. Reusing the same filter slot is the smallest, pattern-consistent change.

**Steps:**
1. In `cmds.log`, after the `flags.type` filter line (925), add: `if (flags.grep) all = all.filter((u) => String(u.title).toLowerCase().includes(String(flags.grep).toLowerCase()));`
2. Leave the rest (slice, reverse, json/plain output) untouched — `--grep` automatically honors `--n`, `--type`, `--reverse`, `--json`.
3. Add the `--grep` token to the help line at bin/chalk.mjs:1028.

**Files:**
- `bin/chalk.mjs` — `cmds.log` (line ~925) + help text (line 1028). Reuse the existing `all.filter` chain pattern.
- `test/protocol.test.mjs` — new test (append after the `--type` test ~line 117).

**Test:** Add `test('log --grep — filters by title substring (case-insensitive), composes with --type; plain unchanged', …)`:
- seed: `update 'deploy api'`, `update 'fix Login bug'`, `update 'deploy worker' --type milestone-hit`.
- `log --grep deploy` includes both deploy lines, excludes `fix Login bug`.
- case-insensitivity: `log --grep LOGIN` includes `fix Login bug`.
- composition: `log --grep deploy --type milestone-hit` shows only `deploy worker`, not `deploy api`.
- `log` (no `--grep`) still shows all three.

This fails today (the `grep` flag is ignored, so `--grep deploy` would show all events including `fix Login bug`) and passes with the filter added.

title: fix: chalk lesson dispatch misroutes lesson text beginning with 'list'

acceptanceCriteria.text: Only the bare `chalk lesson list` (no trailing text) triggers the list subcommand
Recording a lesson whose text starts with the word 'list' records it correctly
A test covers a lesson text beginning with 'list'

issue.body:
`lesson()` treats `_[0] === 'list'` as the list subcommand, so `chalk lesson "list the deps first"` (or `chalk lesson list the deps`) is misrouted to the listing path instead of recording the lesson.

- [ ] Only the bare `chalk lesson list` (no trailing text) triggers the list subcommand
- [ ] Recording a lesson whose text starts with the word 'list' records it correctly
- [ ] A test covers a lesson text beginning with 'list'

_filed by `chalk retro` (self-healing)_

findings.note: The single-word lesson `chalk lesson list` (text being exactly 'list') remains indistinguishable from the bare list subcommand and so cannot be recorded. This is an inherent ambiguity and consistent with criterion 1 (only bare `chalk lesson list` triggers the list subcommand); multi-word texts beginning with 'list' record correctly via the `_.length === 1` guard.

Tests cover multi-word 'list ...' recording (line 50) and bare 'list' listing (line 41), satisfying all criteria. A negative-assertion test confirming the 'list ...' text does NOT print existing lessons would harden coverage, but the file-content assertion is sufficient.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** One-line fix in the `lesson` command dispatch in `bin/chalk.mjs`. The bug: `if (_[0] === 'list')` routes to the list subcommand whenever the first positional token is `list`, so an unquoted lesson like `chalk lesson list comprehensions are great` is swallowed. Gate it on `_.length === 1` so only the bare `chalk lesson list` lists.

**Steps:**
1. In `lesson({ _, flags })`, change the guard from `if (_[0] === 'list')` to `if (_[0] === 'list' && _.length === 1)`.
2. Everything below (text join + `appendLesson`) already handles the record path; multi-token `list ...` now falls through and records correctly.

**Files:**
- `bin/chalk.mjs` — `lesson()` command (~line 887). No new utils; reuses `s.appendLesson` / `s.lessons`.
- `test/protocol.test.mjs` — add a test (mirror the existing `lesson list` test at line 41 for setup helpers `scratch`/`chalk`).

**Test:** Add `test('lesson — text starting with "list" is recorded, not routed to list subcommand')`: `init`, then `chalk(d, 'lesson', 'list', 'comprehensions', 'are', 'great')` exits 0, and assert `.chalk/lessons.md` includes `list comprehensions are great`. This FAILS today (text is misrouted to the list printer, nothing recorded) and passes after the guard change. The existing bare-`lesson list` test (line 41) still guards regression.

title: fix: chalk lesson list cap diverges from the memory injected into agents

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk lesson list` and `buildContext` use the same cap so the listed lessons match what is injected
If a fuller view is intentional, document the distinction and/or gate it behind an explicit flag (e.g. `--all`)
A test asserts the listed lessons correspond to the injected set

issue.body:
`chalk lesson list` reads `s.lessons(1000)` while context injection uses the default `s.lessons(15)`. Criterion 1 promises 'the same memory injected into agents', but the differing cap means `list` can surface older lessons that are never actually injected, misrepresenting what the planner/executor see.

- [ ] `chalk lesson list` and `buildContext` use the same cap so the listed lessons match what is injected
- [ ] If a fuller view is intentional, document the distinction and/or gate it behind an explicit flag (e.g. `--all`)
- [ ] A test asserts the listed lessons correspond to the injected set

_filed by `chalk retro` (self-healing)_

findings.note: The correspondence test relies on regex filtering of 'lesson number N' lines from both outputs; it confirms the default list equals the injected set and that --all differs, which adequately covers criteria 1 and 3. It does not assert the exact cap value (15) or that buildContext and lesson list share a literal constant, but both demonstrably route through store.lessons() with the same default LESSON_CAP, so divergence cannot recur without breaking the test.

Criterion 2 is met via the --all flag plus documentation in code comments (store.mjs:20-21) and help text (chalk.mjs:1029); the distinction between listed/injected vs full history is explicitly described.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** `chalk lesson list` calls `s.lessons(1000)` while `buildContext` calls `store.lessons()` (default cap **15**) — so the list can show lessons that are NOT injected. Fix: make `lesson list` default to the same cap as the injected set, and gate the fuller view behind `--all`. Chosen over silently changing the injected cap, because the 15-cap is the contract agents actually see (criterion 1) and `--all` satisfies the "intentional fuller view" escape hatch (criterion 2). Reuse the existing `Store.lessons(n)` default — no new util.

**Steps:**
1. Extract the injected cap into a single shared constant so both paths reference it (e.g. `LESSON_CAP = 15` in `lib/store.mjs`, used as the default of `lessons(n = LESSON_CAP)` and exported).
2. In `bin/chalk.mjs` `lesson()` `list` branch: use `s.lessons()` (the shared default) instead of `s.lessons(1000)`; when `flags.all` is set, pass a large n to show everything.
3. Update help text (`chalk lesson list [--all]`) to document that `list` mirrors the injected set and `--all` shows the full history.

**Files:**
- `lib/store.mjs` — `lessons(n)` default + exported `LESSON_CAP`; `buildContext` already uses the default.
- `bin/chalk.mjs` — `lesson` command list branch + usage line (~887–890, ~1027).

**Test:** Add to `test/protocol.test.mjs`: add >15 lessons, then assert `chalk lesson list` output line-count equals the lessons in `chalk context` (the injected set) — fails today (list shows all >15, context shows 15) — and assert `chalk lesson list --all` shows the older lesson that the default omits.

title: fix: guard chalk decisions against a missing decisions.md

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk decisions` prints a clean message (e.g. 'no decisions recorded yet.') when `.chalk/decisions.md` is missing or empty
No unhandled exception/stack trace is shown for the missing-file case
A test covers the missing/empty decisions-log branch

issue.body:
`decisions()` in bin/chalk.mjs calls `readFileSync(s.p.decisions, 'utf8')` with no existence guard. If `.chalk/decisions.md` is absent (e.g. an older or hand-edited store) the command throws a raw ENOENT stack instead of a friendly empty-state message, unlike `lesson list` which handles the empty case.

- [ ] `chalk decisions` prints a clean message (e.g. 'no decisions recorded yet.') when `.chalk/decisions.md` is missing or empty
- [ ] No unhandled exception/stack trace is shown for the missing-file case
- [ ] A test covers the missing/empty decisions-log branch

_filed by `chalk retro` (self-healing)_

findings.note: The added test covers the missing-file case but not a present-but-empty/whitespace-only decisions.md; the code's `.trim()` handles it, yet that path is unexercised. Criterion 3 (missing/empty branch) is still satisfied since both resolve to the same '' code path.

A decisions.md containing only the scaffolded header '# Decisions (ADR-lite)' is non-empty after trim, so it prints the bare header rather than 'no decisions recorded yet.' This is acceptable per the criteria (file not truly empty) but is a mild UX edge case.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** Guard the read in the `decisions` command. Two options: (a) inline `existsSync`+empty check in `bin/chalk.mjs`; (b) add a `Store.decisions()` helper mirroring `Store.lessons()`. Pick **(b)** — the lessons-learned explicitly say "reuse the Store helper," it keeps the readFileSync guard centralized with `lessons()`/`updates()`, and the command stays a one-liner.

**Steps:**
1. In `lib/store.mjs`, add a `decisions()` method on `Store` (next to `lessons()`/`updates()`): `return existsSync(this.p.decisions) ? readFileSync(this.p.decisions, 'utf8') : '';`
2. In `bin/chalk.mjs` `decisions()` command, replace the raw `readFileSync(s.p.decisions, 'utf8')` with `const text = s.decisions().trim(); console.log(text || 'no decisions recorded yet.');` — also treats an empty file as "no decisions."
3. No new imports needed (`existsSync` already imported in store.mjs).

**Files:**
- `lib/store.mjs` — add `decisions()` getter (reuse the `existsSync`/`readFileSync` pattern from `lessons()`).
- `bin/chalk.mjs` — `decisions()` command handler (~line 878).

**Test:** In `test/protocol.test.mjs`, add `test('decisions — clean message when log is missing', …)`: `chalk init` only (no `chalk decision`), run `chalk decisions`, assert `r.code === 0`, `r.out` matches `/no decisions recorded yet/`, and that no stack trace/`Error` appears in output. This fails today (unhandled `ENOENT` throw, non-zero exit) and passes with the guard.

title: feat: add explicit `chalk lesson add` subcommand to disambiguate from `list`

acceptanceCriteria.text: `chalk lesson add "<text>"` records the given text as a lesson, including single-word texts like `list`
Bare `chalk lesson list` continues to print the injected lessons (with `--all` for full history)
`chalk lesson "<text>"` (no subcommand) still records as before for back-compat
Help text in bin/chalk.mjs documents the `add` form
A test asserts that a lesson with the exact text `list` is recorded and does not trigger the list subcommand

issue.body:
`lesson()` in bin/chalk.mjs dispatches the `list` subcommand via `_[0] === 'list' && _.length === 1`, and otherwise treats `_.join(' ')` as the lesson text. This leaves an inherent ambiguity: the single-word lesson text `chalk lesson list` is indistinguishable from the bare `list` subcommand and therefore cannot be recorded. Adding an explicit `add` subcommand (while keeping the bare-positional form for back-compat) removes the collision entirely.

- [ ] `chalk lesson add "<text>"` records the given text as a lesson, including single-word texts like `list`
- [ ] Bare `chalk lesson list` continues to print the injected lessons (with `--all` for full history)
- [ ] `chalk lesson "<text>"` (no subcommand) still records as before for back-compat
- [ ] Help text in bin/chalk.mjs documents the `add` form
- [ ] A test asserts that a lesson with the exact text `list` is recorded and does not trigger the list subcommand

_filed by `chalk retro` (self-healing)_

findings.note: Criterion 5 test (line 80-87) correctly asserts `chalk lesson add list` records `^- list` verbatim and not via the `add list` free-text fallback. Edge cases for the add branch with no payload (`die` path) are not directly tested, but this is minor and the usage guard is straightforward.

plan:
## Plan

**Approach:** Add an explicit `add` subcommand branch to `cmds.lesson` in `bin/chalk.mjs`, mirroring the existing `task add` dispatch pattern. When `_[0] === 'add'`, record `_.slice(1).join(' ')` verbatim so single-word payloads like `list` are stored, never dispatched. The bare `list` branch (L888) and the free-text fallback (L896) stay untouched for back-compat. Reuses `s.appendLesson`/`die`/`ok` — no new structure, and satisfies the lesson about giving an overloaded positional an explicit subcommand.

**Steps:**
1. In `cmds.lesson` (~L887, before the `list` branch) add: `if (_[0] === 'add') { const text = _.slice(1).join(' ') || flags.text; if (!text) die('usage: chalk lesson add "<text>"'); s.appendLesson({ lesson: text, by: flags.by || 'human' }); return ok(...); }`.
2. Leave the `_[0] === 'list' && _.length === 1` branch and the trailing free-text fallback exactly as-is (criteria 2 & 3).
3. Add a help line at ~L1029 documenting `chalk lesson add "<text>"` next to the existing `lesson` / `lesson list` lines (criterion 4).

**Files:**
- `bin/chalk.mjs` — `cmds.lesson` handler (~L886) + the help block (~L1029). Reuse `s.appendLesson`, `die`, `ok`.

**Test (add to `test/protocol.test.mjs`, near the L73 "list" test):** Run `chalk(d, 'lesson', 'add', 'list')`, assert `.code === 0`, and assert `.chalk/lessons.md` matches `/^- list\b/m` (exact text `list` recorded, not `add list`). Without the branch, `add` is treated as free text and the recorded line is `- add list …` → `/^- list\b/m` FAILS; with the branch it records `- list …` and passes. (Criterion 5.)

title: fix: make pipeline stages idempotent so an interrupted sweep resumes cleanly

acceptanceCriteria.text: A hermetic test that runs the pipeline to a partial point, then re-runs and asserts it resumes — **no duplicate branch, no duplicate commit** — and the task still reaches `done` (compose the assertion across stages, per the recorded lesson; don't test one stage in isolation).
Existing 53 tests stay green; the locked suite is untouched.
No change to the happy-path ORDER; only the per-stage 'already done?' guards.

issue.body:
## Problem
When an autopilot sweep is interrupted mid-pipeline (process killed, crash, rate-limit), the partial work is left in place — a half-built branch + worktree. On the next run the `branch`/`plan`/`commit` stages **re-run blindly and report `ok` again** instead of detecting the existing work and resuming. This was observed directly: a killed #34 run left `feat/34-…` + its worktree, and the relaunch logged `branch: ok` without noticing the branch already existed.

This is the prerequisite for any unattended *standing* loop: an interrupted run MUST resume without duplicating branches/commits or wedging.

## Acceptance criteria
1. The `branch` stage detects an existing local branch/worktree for the task and **reuses** it (no error, no duplicate) instead of recreating.
2. The `commit` stage no-ops when there is nothing new to commit (already committed) rather than failing the sweep.
3. Re-running the pipeline on a task that is already partway through resumes from the first *incomplete* stage; completed stages are skipped/idempotent.

## Checklist (definition of done)
- [ ] A hermetic test that runs the pipeline to a partial point, then re-runs and asserts it resumes — **no duplicate branch, no duplicate commit** — and the task still reaches `done` (compose the assertion across stages, per the recorded lesson; don't test one stage in isolation).
- [ ] Existing 53 tests stay green; the locked suite is untouched.
- [ ] No change to the happy-path ORDER; only the per-stage 'already done?' guards.

findings.note: The `review` stage (part of the pipeline ORDER, line 10 of lib/pipeline.mjs) was NOT made idempotent. The review() handler (bin/chalk.mjs:749) never sets t.pipeline.stage='reviewed' (rank 6 in PIPE_STAGES is dead/unused) and has no stageDone guard. Every other stage (branch/plan/work/commit/pr/evidence) got a guard, but on an interrupted sweep that is resumed with a reviewer configured, the adversarial reviewer is re-invoked and a DUPLICATE review record is appended to t.reviews each time. The stated goal is 'make pipeline stages idempotent so an interrupted sweep resumes cleanly' — this stage is not idempotent, so the goal is only partially met.

The resume test (pipeline.test.mjs:318) configures neither a reviewer nor an e2e runner, so both the `review` and `evidence` stages are SKIPPED during the resumed sweep. Criterion 1 explicitly demands composing the assertion across stages and not testing stages in isolation, yet the idempotency/resume behavior of review and evidence is never exercised. The happy-path-only partial point (pr-open) leaves the post-pr-open stages' resume paths unverified.

The `merge` stage (bin/chalk.mjs:364) has no stageDone idempotency guard; it relies solely on the pipeline queue filtering out state==='done' tasks plus the 'already MERGED' catch. If a merge is interrupted after `gh pr merge` succeeds but before state is persisted to 'done', a resume re-invokes merge and re-runs verify/review gates against a torn-down/merged branch, which is fragile compared with the explicit guards added to the other stages.

Cannot confirm criterion 2 ('existing 53 tests stay green; locked suite untouched') from static inspection — the new test was added into pipeline.test.mjs and the guards alter exit-codes/no-op behavior of branch/commit/pr/plan/work/evidence; verify+test-integrity must be confirmed green, which is not evidenced here.

The idempotency test exercises a single interrupt point (pr-open) before resuming. Other interrupt boundaries (e.g. interrupted right after branch, or after commit but before pr) are not directly exercised, though the monotonic-rank guard design makes them behave uniformly and the review stage has its own dedicated resume test.

Stage state for fresh tasks relies on stageRank(undefined) === -1; 'selected' (rank 0) is defined in PIPE_STAGES but never appears to be assigned, so the first guard depends on the undefined→-1 fallback. Behavior is correct but slightly implicit.

plan:
I have a complete picture. Here's the plan.

---

**Approach:** Add a single monotonic stage-rank guard and apply it at the entry of each side-effecting pipeline stage so re-running a stage that's already at/past its target no-ops with exit 0 instead of re-doing the side effect (duplicate `git commit`, duplicate `gh pr create`) or dying ("nothing to commit") and blocking the task. The happy-path ORDER in `lib/pipeline.mjs` is unchanged — only per-stage "already done?" guards are added, exactly as criterion 3 requires. (Alternative: track booleans per side effect — rejected; the existing `t.pipeline.stage` already encodes monotonic progress and is the minimal reuse.)

**Steps:**
1. In `bin/chalk.mjs`, add a module-level helper near `now()`: `PIPE_STAGES = ['selected','branched','planned','verified','committed','pr-open','reviewed','tested','cleaned']`, `stageRank(st)`, and `stageDone(t, target) = stageRank(t.pipeline?.stage) >= stageRank(target)`.
2. At the top of each command (after `mustTask`), early-return `ok('… (already done)')` when `stageDone`: `branch`→`'branched'`, `plan`→`'planned'`, `work`→`'verified'`, `commit`→`'committed'`, `pr`→`'pr-open'`, `evidence`→`'tested'`. (`merge` already self-guards via `state !== 'in-progress'` + the done-filter in the queue.)
3. Keep all stage bodies and the `ORDER` array untouched so the order/skip logic is identical.

**Files:**
- `bin/chalk.mjs` — add `stageDone`/`stageRank` helper; guard `branch` (247), `plan` (320), `work` (343), `commit` (268), `pr` (299), `evidence` (506). Reuse existing `t.pipeline.stage`, `ok()`, `now()`.
- `test/pipeline.test.mjs` — new hermetic test (reuse `repoWithBare`, `stubGh`, executor stub pattern from the existing `pipeline —` test).

**Test:** Add `pipeline — idempotent stages: an interrupted sweep resumes with no duplicate branch/commit/PR`. Drive a task *partially* to `pr-open` via individual commands (`branch`→executor write→`work`→`commit`→`pr`), with the stub `gh` appending to a file on every `pr create`. Then run full `chalk pipeline` and assert (composed across stages, per the lesson): exactly **one** feature commit on the branch (`git log` count), `gh pr create` recorded **exactly once** (no duplicate PR), `branchExists` true only for the single branch, and the task reaches `state === 'done'` / `pipeline.stage === 'cleaned'`. Without the guards this FAILS — re-running `commit` dies "nothing to commit" (blocks the task) and/or `pr` files a duplicate PR; with them it resumes cleanly to `done`.
